items.blurb: Brunson ties it late as Knicks force overtime in Game 1 vs. Cavaliers — Jalen Brunson ties Game 1 at 101-101 with a clutch bucket late in the fourth quarter before the Cavs miss a last-second chance, sending the Eastern Conference Finals opener to overtime.

items.headline: Cavaliers' 'hacking' strategy pays off in Game 1 loss — until it didn't
items.blurb: Cavaliers' 'hacking' strategy pays off in Game 1 loss — until it didn't — Leading by double digits in Game 1, the Cleveland Cavaliers made the counterintuitive decision to start sending the New York Knicks to the foul line. Not just any Knick — 40.8 percent free-throw shooter Mitchell Robinson.

items.headline: Matt Riccardi Leaving Mavericks' Front Office
items.blurb: Matt Riccardi Leaving Mavericks' Front Office — Matt Riccardi is leaving the Dallas Mavericks' front office as the franchise changes direction under Masai Ujiri. Riccardi served as co-general manager of the Mavericks, alongside Michael Finely, following the firing of Nico Harrison. items.blurb: Trajan Langdon Says Pistons Want To Re-Sign Jalen Duren, Tobias Harris — Detroit Pistons general manager Trajan Langdon said Tuesday the club intends to pursue contract extensions with both All-Star center Jalen Duren and veteran forward Tobias Harris following a 60-win season that ended in a Game 7 second-round loss to the Cleveland Cavaliers. items.headline: Cavaliers’ Mitchell absorbs contact, drains triple for four-point play
items.blurb: Cavaliers’ Mitchell absorbs contact, drains triple for four-point play — The Cleveland Cavaliers’ Donovan Mitchell, in the first half of Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals, takes a bump from the New York Knicks’ Mikal Bridges and hits the improbable three-pointer before making his free-throw for a four-point play.

items.headline: Jason Kidd, Mavericks Agree To Part Ways
items.blurb: Jason Kidd, Mavericks Agree To Part Ways — Jason Kidd and the Dallas Mavericks have mutually agreed to part ways. Kidd had four seasons and approximately $40 million remaining on his contract but exits the Mavericks a few months after the franchise fired Nico Harrison as general manager. items.blurb: Matt Lloyd, Mike Gansey, Nick U'Ren Among Candidates For Sixers' Lead Basketball Exec Job — Matt Lloyd, Mike Gansey and Nick U'Ren are among the candidates for the Philadelphia 76ers lead basketball executive job to replace Daryl Morey. items.headline: Sean Sweeney Considered Candidate For Bulls, Magic Head Coaching Jobs
items.blurb: Sean Sweeney Considered Candidate For Bulls, Magic Head Coaching Jobs — Sean Sweeney has emerged as a head coaching candidate for both the Chicago Bulls and Orlando Magic. Sweeney is currently the lead assistant coach for the San Antonio Spurs after previously holding the same role with the Dallas Mavericks. items.blurb: Magic Have Billy Donovan, Jeff Van Gundy, Sean Sweeney On Head Coach Candidate List — The Orlando Magic are beginning to advance their search for a new head coach to replace Jahmal Mosley, sources tell Jake Fischer. items.headline: De'Aaron Fox: Game-time call for Game 2
items.blurb: De'Aaron Fox: Game-time call for Game 2 — Fox (ankle) is a game-time decision for Wednesday's Game 2 of the Western Conference Finals against the Thunder, per Tom Orsborn of the San Antonio Express-News. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama draws attention with pregame routine — Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs duked it out against the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals on Monday, and while the Thunder are largely expected to take this series, by the looks of the first game, it won't be easy.

items.headline: LeBron James Has Already Received Interest From Most Rival Contenders
items.blurb: LeBron James Has Already Received Interest From Most Rival Contenders — LeBron James has yet to determine whether he will play a 24th NBA season, but he continues to receive significant interest across the league at the age of 41. items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama's historic Game 1 performance lifts Spurs past Thunder in 2OT thriller — In what was viewed by many as the real NBA Finals coming in, the San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder put on a show for the ages in a Game 1 double-overtime thriller in the Western Conference Finals on Monday night.

items.blurb: Bucks Expected To Acquire Additional 2026 NBA Draft Picks — There is an expectation from the Milwaukee Bucks that they will have multiple selections in this year's draft, rival teams tell ESPN's Jeremy Woo. items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo Continues To Prefer Trade From Bucks — Giannis Antetokounmpo continues to signal to the Milwaukee Bucks that he is ready to move on from the franchise this offseason via trade. items.blurb: NBA Teams Still Waiting On Results Of Darryn Peterson's Medicals — Darryn Peterson's medical evaluations have not yet been shared with NBA teams. Those results remain the most closely watched off-court variable ahead of the NBA Draft, as teams assess the cramping issues that hampered his freshman season at Kansas. items.blurb: AJ Dybantsa Not Considered Lock To Be Picked At No. 1 By Wizards — AJ Dybantsa is not currently viewed as a lock to go first overall to the Washington Wizards, according to rival teams. Unlike some drafts, there is no true consensus top prospect in the 2026 NBA draft as arguments can be made for Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cam Boozer and Caleb Wilson. items.headline: Donovan Mitchell: Paces Cleveland in loss
items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell: Paces Cleveland in loss — Mitchell finished Tuesday's 115-104 overtime loss to the Knicks in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals with 29 points (12-23 FG, 4-11 3Pt, 1-1 FT), six steals, five rebounds, three assists and one block across 41 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson: Takes over late in Game 1 win
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson: Takes over late in Game 1 win — Brunson totaled 38 points (15-29 FG, 1-6 3Pt, 7-10 FT), six assists, five rebounds and three steals across 46 minutes in Tuesday's 115-104 overtime victory over the Cavaliers in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: OG Anunoby: Ready for Game 1 — Anunoby (hamstring) is available for Tuesday's Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals against the Cavaliers, per James L. Edwards III of The Athletic. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
